type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both files fully overlap since our features touch the same Editor methods (edit_file/edit_files) and click.edit() signature. I'll own both files and implement your process_priority feature too. Please send a SPEC with exact param name, type, default, and behavior (subprocess priority mechanism per OS) so I can merge it in alongside multi-file support.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py
spec: In Editor class: rename edit_file(self, filename: str) -> None to edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None. It should build the subprocess command by joining the editor with each filename individually quoted, e.g.: filenames = list(filenames); editor = self.get_editor(); ... c = subprocess.Popen(f'{editor} ' + ' '.join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames), env=environ, shell=True) - preserve existing try/except OSError and non-zero exit_code handling (same ClickException messages). In Editor.edit(), update the call site from self.edit_file(name) to self.edit_files([name]) (still single temp file, wrapped in a list). The only required behavioral change is that edit_files accepts an iterable and passes multiple quoted filenames to the shell command.
note: Needed for click.edit(filename=...) to accept an iterable of filenames

In Editor.edit(), update the call site from self.edit_file(name) to self.edit_files([name]) (still single temp file, wrapped in a list). The only required behavioral change is that edit_files accepts an iterable and passes multiple quoted filenames to the shell command.
